title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: anumaan R package preprocessing antimicrobial resistance (AMR) surveillance data. standardizes messy hospital data analysis-ready formats proper normalization, classification, quality control.

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: Automated pipeline: Process entire datasets one function Organism normalization: Handles typos, abbreviations, variants Antibiotic classification: AWaRe categories MDR/XDR classification: /CDC-compliant definitions Event deduplication: Create unique infection episodes Data validation: Comprehensive quality checks

title: Getting Started with anumaan
code: # From source during development devtools::install()  # Or install from package file install.packages("anumaan_0.1.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: Getting Started with anumaan
code: library(anumaan) library(dplyr)

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: simplest way use anumaan amr_preprocess() function:
code: # Load your data library(readxl) raw_data <- read_excel("your_data.xlsx")  # Run complete preprocessing result <- amr_preprocess(   data = raw_data,   config = amr_config(     hai_cutoff = 2, # Days to classify HAI     mdr_definition = "CDC", # MDR criteria     fuzzy_match = TRUE # Auto-detect columns   ),   verbose = TRUE,   validate = TRUE,   generate_report = TRUE )  # Get processed data clean_data <- result$data  # View summary summary(result)

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: Phase 1: Standardization - Maps column names automatically - Normalizes organism names (handles typos) - Normalizes antibiotic names + AWaRe classification - Standardizes specimen types - Parses dates - Cleans R/S/values Phase 2: Enrichment - Calculates age DOB - Computes length stay - Classifies HAI vs CAI - Infers missing hospital departments Phase 3: Derivation - Creates event IDs (deduplication) - Classifies MDR/XDR - Flags contaminants - Calculates polymicrobial weights - Maps GBD pathogens

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: Let’s create minimal example:
code: # Create sample hospital data sample_data <- data.frame(   PatientID = c(1, 1, 2, 3),   Age = c(65, 65, 34, 78),   Gender = c("M", "M", "F", "M"),   SampleDate = as.Date(c("2024-01-15", "2024-01-15", "2024-01-20", "2024-02-01")),   Specimen = c("Blood", "Blood", "Urine", "Blood"),   Organism = c("E. coli", "S. aureus", "E. coli", "K. pneumoniae"),   Antibiotic = c("Ampicillin", "Oxacillin", "Ciprofloxacin", "Meropenem"),   Result = c("R", "S", "S", "R") )  # Process it result <- amr_preprocess(sample_data, verbose = FALSE)  # View results head(result$data)

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: can also use functions separately custom workflows:

title: Getting Started with anumaan
code: # Normalize organism names data <- data.frame(   organism = c("E. coli", "ACINETOBACTER BAUMANNII", "K. pneumoniae") )  normalized <- normalize_organism(   data,   organism_col = "organism",   add_organism_group = TRUE )  # View results normalized[, c("organism", "organism_normalized", "organism_group")]

title: Getting Started with anumaan
code: # Add antibiotic classes and AWaRe categories data <- data.frame(   antibiotic = c("Ampicillin", "Meropenem", "Ciprofloxacin") )  normalized <- normalize_antibiotic(   data,   antibiotic_col = "antibiotic" )  # View AWaRe classification normalized[, c("antibiotic", "antibiotic_normalized", "aware_category")]

title: Getting Started with anumaan
code: # Classify multidrug resistance # (Requires normalized organism and antibiotic names) classified <- classify_mdr(   processed_data,   organism_col = "organism_normalized",   antibiotic_col = "antibiotic_normalized",   susceptibility_col = "antibiotic_value" )  # Check MDR status table(classified$mdr_status)

title: Getting Started with anumaan
code: # Convert wide format to long long_data <- pivot_wide_to_long(   wide_data,   id_cols = c("PatientID", "Age", "Gender"),   antibiotic_cols = NULL # Auto-detect antibiotic columns )  # Aggregate to organism-antibiotic level aggregated <- collapse_to_antibiotic_level(   processed_data,   organism_col = "organism_normalized",   antibiotic_col = "antibiotic_normalized",   susceptibility_col = "antibiotic_value" )

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: Customize pipeline amr_config():
code: config <- amr_config(   # Column mappings (auto-detects common variants)   fuzzy_match = TRUE,    # Time windows   hai_cutoff = 2, # Days for HAI classification   event_gap_days = 14, # Event deduplication window   mortality_window = 14, # Death attribution window    # Age grouping   age_bins = "GBD_standard", # Or custom: c(0, 18, 45, 65, Inf)    # Resistance definitions   mdr_definition = "CDC", # Or "WHO" or numeric threshold   xdr_definition = "CDC",    # Normalization options   intermediate_as_resistant = TRUE, # Treat I as R    # Validation   strict_validation = FALSE # Warning vs error on issues )  # Use custom config result <- amr_preprocess(raw_data, config = config)

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: anumaan includes validation functions:
code: # Check required fields validation <- validate_required_fields(   raw_data,   required_cols = c("PatientID", "Organism", "Antibiotic", "Result"),   min_completeness = 0.8 )  # View validation results validation$valid validation$messages  # Check data quality quality <- validate_data_quality(   result$data,   check_duplicates = TRUE,   check_outliers = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with anumaan
text: Run specific phases:
code: # Only standardization (no enrichment or derivation) result <- amr_preprocess(   raw_data,   phases = "standardize",   verbose = TRUE )  # Standardization + Enrichment (no derivation) result <- amr_preprocess(   raw_data,   phases = c("standardize", "enrich"),   verbose = TRUE )

code: amr_config(   column_mappings = NULL,   fuzzy_match = TRUE,   strict_validation = FALSE,   date_columns = c("date_of_admission", "date_of_culture", "date_of_final_outcome",     "DOB"),   hai_cutoff = 2,   infer_department = TRUE,   event_gap_days = 14,   mortality_window = 14,   age_bins = "GBD_standard",   contaminant_method = "auto",   mdr_definition = "CDC",   xdr_definition = "CDC",   map_icd10 = TRUE,   rr_table = "GBD_2021",   organism_map = "default",   antibiotic_map = "WHO_2023",   intermediate_as_resistant = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create AMR Preprocessing Configuration — amr_config
text: column_mappings Named list column name mappings. Default uses default_column_mappings. fuzzy_match Logical. Enable fuzzy matching column names. Default TRUE. strict_validation Logical. TRUE, stops execution required fields missing. FALSE, issues warnings. Default FALSE. date_columns Character vector date column names parse. Default includes standard date fields. hai_cutoff Numeric. Number days admission classify Hospital-Acquired Infection (HAI). Default 2. infer_department Logical. Attempt infer hospital department variables missing. Default TRUE. event_gap_days Numeric. Minimum days events create new event_id. Default 14. mortality_window Numeric. Days culture classify death infection-related. Default 14. age_bins Character. Age binning strategy: "GBD_standard", "pediatric", "geriatric". Can also custom vector. Default "GBD_standard". contaminant_method Character. Method contaminant classification: "auto" (cascade available methods), "device_based", "heuristic", "provided". Default "auto". mdr_definition Character numeric. MDR definition: "CDC", "", numeric threshold number resistant classes. Default "CDC". xdr_definition Character numeric. XDR definition. Default "CDC". map_icd10 Logical. Attempt ICD-10 code mapping. Default TRUE. rr_table Character. Name built-RR table use, path custom RR table. Default "GBD_2021". organism_map Character named vector. "default" built-mapping, custom named vector. Default "default". antibiotic_map Character. Built-antibiotic classification: "WHO_2023" path custom. Default "WHO_2023". intermediate_as_resistant Logical. Treat Intermediate () Resistant (R) except special cases like Colistin. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages processing. Default TRUE.

text: Data frame columns syndrome (= syndrome_name),   adjustment_type, CR_L, (facility_col   supplied) facility_col. Additional columns   (n_inpatient, n_total, etc.) give raw counts used.

title: Calculate the CFR adjustment factor (CR_L) — calculate_CR_L
text: Three adjustment types supported: None CR_L = 1. hospital CFR applies directly (e.g., BSI,     Meningitis, hospital-acquired infections). Inpatient ratio CR_L = (patients \(\ge\) 1 inpatient visit) / (patients).     Used community cases captured partly outpatient data     (e.g., community-acquired LRI, UTI). Outpatient inpatient ratio CR_L = (patients \(\ge\) 1 outpatient \(\ge\) 1 inpatient     visit) / (patients \(\ge\) 1 outpatient visit).     Used syndromes OP--IP transition captures disease severity     (e.g., STI, Skin, Eye, Oral, Bone/joint infections).

title: Calculate Fatal Pathogen Distribution (P_{LK}) — calculate_P_Lk

Unique patient identifier column. pathogen_col Character. Pathogen (organism) column (K). outcome_col Character. Final patient outcome column. death_value Character. Value indicating fatal outcome. Default "Death". specimen_col Character NULL. Specimen type column. supplied, specimen_name must also provided. specimen_name Character NULL. title: Calculate Fatal Pathogen Distribution (P_{LK}) — calculate_P_Lk
text: named list: P_Lk Data frame pooled P_LK across facilities. Columns:     pathogen_col, N_F_LK (weighted fatal patient count K),     N_F_L (total fatal patients L), P_Lk (fraction). facility_level Per-facility P_LK data frame (    facility_col supplied facility_name     NULL).

title: Calculate Fatal Pathogen Distribution (P_{LK}) — calculate_P_Lk
text: Unit analysis: patient. fatal patient contributes total weight 1, split equally across valid pathogens: $$w_{r,k} = \frac{1}{m_r}$$ \(m_r\) number distinct pathogens patient \(r\). Monomicrobial patients (\(m_r = 1\)) receive weight 1; polymicrobial patients death split equally (Approach 1). Pooled formula across facilities: $$P_{LK}^{\text{pooled}} =   \frac{\sum_f N^{F}_{f,L,K}}{\sum_f N^{F}_{f,L}}$$

title: Calculate Fatal Pathogen Distribution (P_{LK}) — calculate_P_Lk

title: Calculate fatal pathogen distribution (P_{Lk}) — calculate_P_Lk_fatal
text: unit analysis patient. patient contributes total weight 1, distributed equally across valid pathogens. patient \(m_r\) valid pathogens, pathogen receives weight \(1/m_r\). polymicrobial patients (polymicrobial_col == 1), pathogens listed GLASS reference (glass_ref) given specimen type retained weighting. Monomicrobial patients (polymicrobial_col == 0) never filtered. pooled formula across facilities : $$P_{LK}^{\text{pooled}} =   \frac{\sum_f N^{F}_{f,L,K}}{\sum_f N^{F}_{f,L}}$$

title: Calculate non-fatal pathogen distribution (P'_{Lk}) — calculate_P_Lk_prime
text: unit analysis patient. patient contributes total weight 1, distributed equally across valid pathogens. patient \(m_r\) valid pathogens, pathogen receives weight \(1/m_r\). polymicrobial patients (polymicrobial_col == 1), pathogens listed GLASS reference (glass_ref) given specimen type retained weighting. Monomicrobial patients (polymicrobial_col == 0) never filtered. pooled formula across facilities : $$P'_{LK}^{\text{pooled}} =   \frac{\sum_f N^{NF}_{f,L,K}}{\sum_f N^{NF}_{f,L}}$$

title: Calculate YLD per pathogen — calculate_YLD
text: $$YLD_K = \text{Incidence}_L \times P'_{LK} \times \text{DW\_sepsis}$$ YLD weight per incident case drawn GBD-derived reference table stratified Indian state (yld_ref) DW_sepsis provided. DW_sepsis provided directly, scalar used rows yld_ref ignored. Facility-level mode (facility_col supplied facility_name NULL): P_Lk_prime_tbl must contain facility_col column (.e., facility_level element calculate_P_Lk_prime()). incidence_data must data frame facility_col incidence count column. facility_state_map data frame linking facility state required DW_sepsis supplied. Pooled / -facility mode: incidence_data single numeric scalar P_Lk_prime_tbl facility column. state_name selects YLD weight; defaults "India" NULL.

title: Calculate Deaths by Infectious Syndrome (D_L) — calculate_syndrome_deaths
text: Three operating modes, evaluated priority order: Pre-computed components (HIGH confidence): pass outputs     calculate_deaths_by_cause(), calculate_infection_fraction(),     calculate_syndrome_fraction() via d_j, s_j,     m_lj. Computes: D_L = sum_J(D_J * S_J * M_LJ). Raw population data (HIGH confidence): pass pop_data     three components computed internally combining.     Computes: D_L = sum_J(D_J * S_J * M_LJ). Facility fallback (LOW confidence): population     inputs available, counts number unique patients     died syndrome directly facility_data. Use syndrome restrict results single syndrome interest. Use facility_col facility_name restrict facility fallback specific site.

title: Compute Fatal Prevalence of Resistance (R_kd) — compute_R_kd_fatal

text: profiles_with_rr Named list assign_rr_to_profiles() called rr_col = "OR_death". probability_col Character.  Profile probability column. Default "probability". rr_profile_col Character.  Profile-level column produced assign_rr_to_profiles().  Default "RR_LOS_profile".

title: Compute Fatal Prevalence of Resistance (R_kd) — compute_R_kd_fatal
text: Named list (one entry per pathogen) containing: per_profile: profile data frame augmented       numerator_R_kd (= \(R'_{K\delta} \cdot \text{}_{K\delta}\))       R_kd (= numerator / E[OR_k]) per profile. R_k: overall fatal prevalence resistance pathogen k       (sum R_kd resistant profiles ). E_OR_k: expected =       \(\sum_\delta R'_{K\delta} \cdot \text{}_{K\delta}\).

title: Compute Fatal Prevalence of Resistance (R_kd) — compute_R_kd_fatal
text: $$E[\text{}_k] = \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta} \cdot \text{}_{K\delta}$$ $$R_{kd} = \frac{R'_{K\delta} \cdot \text{}_{K\delta}}{E[\text{}_k]}$$ $$R_{k} = \sum_{\delta \neq \delta_0} R_{kd}$$ \(R'_{K\delta}\) profile probability compute_resistance_profiles() \(\text{}_{K\delta}\) assigned assign_rr_to_profiles() rr_col = "OR_death" (-susceptible profile carries = 1). Usage pipeline:
code: # 1. Fit mortality OR per class   mort_or <- fit_mortality_rr_logistic(data, ...)    # 2. Assign OR to profiles via max rule   profiles_with_or <- assign_rr_to_profiles(       profiles_output,       rr_table = mort_or,       rr_col   = "OR_death"   )    # 3. Compute R_kd (fatal prevalence of resistance)   rkd <- compute_R_kd_fatal(profiles_with_or)

title: Compute Base YLL Block from a Scalar D_L (Steps 1-3) — compute_base_yll_from_dl
text: dl Numeric scalar. \(D_L\), number deaths syndrome L (e.g. 45.2 deaths per 1000 incidences). patient_data Data frame. Patient-level records (one row per patient x antibiotic test per patient x pathogen). Used derive observed age (x sex) proportions death cohort. patient_col Character. Unique patient identifier column patient_data. outcome_col Character. Final-outcome column patient_data. death_value Character (scalar vector). Value(s) outcome_col indicating fatal outcome. Default "Death". age_bin_col Character. Column patient_data containing GBD-standard age bin labels (e.g. "0-1", "1-5", ..., "85+").  Use age_bin_map recode non-standard labels (default remaps "<1" -> "0-1"). sex_col Character. Sex column patient_data. syndrome_col Character NULL. Syndrome column patient_data (e.g. "infectious_syndrome").  Required syndrome_name NULL. syndrome_name Character NULL. supplied, patient_data filtered rows syndrome_col == syndrome_name computing proportions outputs (e.g. "Bloodstream infection"). NULL uses syndromes. pathogen_col Character NULL. Pathogen (organism) column. supplied, per-pathogen age proportions computed per_pathogen by_pathogen_age_sex outputs populated. facility_col Character NULL. Facility identifier column. supplied, per-facility proportions computed by_facility populated. facility_name Character NULL. provided, filters patient_data specified facility computation. use_sex Logical. Whether disaggregate sex well age (uses sex-specific life expectancy). Default TRUE. le_path Character. Path India life expectancy xlsx file. Defaults bundled inst/extdata copy. male_value Character. Value sex_col males. Default "Male". female_value Character. Value sex_col females. Default "Female".  values map "Combined". age_bin_map Named character vector. Remaps non-standard age bin labels joining life table.  Default c("<1" = "0-1"). stratify_by Character vector NULL. Additional columns patient_data include stratified output.

title: Compute Base YLL Block from a Scalar D_L (Steps 1-3) — compute_base_yll_from_dl
text: named list: total Scalar: total base YLL = \(\sum_x D_L^x e_*^x\). by_age_sex Data frame: base YLL age bin x sex. per_pathogen Data frame: base YLL per pathogen K, computed     using pathogen's death age distribution (    pathogen_col supplied). by_pathogen_age_sex Data frame: base YLL pathogen x     age bin x sex (pathogen_col supplied). by_facility Data frame: base YLL facility (    facility_col supplied). by_syndrome_pathogen Data frame: base YLL syndrome x     pathogen (syndrome_col pathogen_col     supplied). stratified Data frame: base YLL aggregated     stratify_by (stratify_by supplied). disaggregated_dl Data frame: full expanded table     columns D_x_L (\(D_L^x\)), proportion (\(\hat{p}_x\)),     life_expectancy (\(e_*^x\)), yll_contribution     (\(D_L^x \times e_*^x\)) every age x sex stratum.     row-level audit trail.

title: Compute Base YLL Block from a Scalar D_L (Steps 1-3) — compute_base_yll_from_dl
text: Formula: $$   \text{base\_YLL}_L = \sum_x D_L^x \, e_*^x $$ $$D_L^x = D_L \times \hat{p}_x$$ \(\hat{p}_x\) observed proportion deaths age bin \(x\) (within sex \(s\) use_sex = TRUE), estimated patient_data (filtered deaths, optionally syndrome_name). D_L note: 1000-incidence normalisation: $$D_L = 1000 \times \text{death\_rate}_L,\quad   \text{death\_rate}_L = \frac{\#\text{deaths}\mid L}{\#\text{incidence}_L}$$ caller supplies scalar directly via dl. Per-pathogen / per-facility YLL: age (x sex) proportions re-computed within subgroup subgroup's YLL reflects age structure, scaled shared dl.

title: Compute Base YLL Block from a Scalar D_L (Steps 1-3) — compute_base_yll_from_dl
text: Bhaswati Ganguli. DALY Methodology AMR (YLD notes). March 2026. Antimicrobial Resistance Collaborators. Global burden bacterial antimicrobial resistance 2019. Lancet. 2022.

Profile prevalence column R'_Kdelta. Default "probability". rr_profile_col Character. Dominant-class converted RR. Default "RR_LOS_profile". dominant_class_col Character. title: Compute Per-Profile Fatal Prevalence of Resistance R_Kdelta* (Eq. 13) — compute_fatal_resistance_prevalence
text: Named list (one per pathogen).  element: per_profile Data frame resistant profiles augmented     R_star (R*_Kdelta per profile) numerator_delta. R_K_star Scalar: \(\sum_\delta R^*_{K\delta}\) =     total fatal resistance prevalence pathogen K. sum_r_prime Scalar: \(\sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}\)     (resistant profiles ). susceptible_fraction Scalar:     \(1 - \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}\). denominator Scalar: shared denominator profiles. n_resistant_profiles Integer: number resistant profiles.

title: Compute Per-Profile Fatal Prevalence of Resistance R_Kdelta* (Eq. 13) — compute_fatal_resistance_prevalence
text: $$   R^*_{K\delta} =     \frac{R'_{K\delta} \cdot RR_{Kd^*}}          {\left(1 - \textstyle\sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}\right)           + \textstyle\sum_\delta R'_{K\delta} \cdot RR_{Kd^*}} $$ sums \(\sum_\delta\) run resistant profiles (profiles least one resistant antibiotic class, .e. dominant_class != "all_susceptible").  term \((1 - \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta})\) susceptible fraction must > 0 formula give R*_Kdelta < 1. Important: pass output assign_rr_to_profiles() directly – call filter_profiles_to_rr_classes() first, renormalisation set \(\sum R'_{K\delta} = 1\) collapse denominator, producing R*_Kdelta = 1 every pathogen.

title: Compute Associated-Burden Fractions per Resistance Profile — compute_fraction_associated
text: single drug-class d formula simplifies : Fraction_assoc_Kd = R'_Kd * RR_Kd / [(1 - R'_Kd) + R'_Kd * RR_Kd] resistance profiles denominator becomes expected RR across profiles (including -susceptible profile RR = 1): E_RR_k = sum_delta  R'_K_delta * RR_K_delta          = 1 + sum_delta  R'_K_delta * (RR_K_delta - 1)   [equivalent] Per-profile associated fraction:   fraction_K_delta = R'_K_delta * RR_K_delta / E_RR_k Overall associated fraction (resistant profiles combined):   Fraction_k = sum_{delta != 0}  fraction_K_delta delta != 0 denotes profiles least one resistant class. Note: E_RR_k numerically identical `denominator` produced compute_paf_los() – equal 1 + sum_d R'_kd*(RR_kd - 1).

title: Compute Mortality Population Attributable Fraction per Resistance Profile — compute_paf_rr_mortality
text: $$\text{PAF}_{kd,\text{mort}} =   \frac{R'_{K\delta}\,(\text{}_{K\delta} - 1)}        {1 + \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}\,(\text{}_{K\delta} - 1)}$$ -susceptible profile carries = 1 contributes 0. denominator equals \(E[\text{}_k] = \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta} \cdot \text{}_{K\delta}\), numerically identical denominator produced compute_paf_los(). Overall mortality PAF pathogen k: $$\text{PAF}_{k,\text{mort}} = \sum_\delta \text{PAF}_{kd,\text{mort}}   = \frac{\sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}(\text{}_{K\delta}-1)}          {1 + \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}(\text{}_{K\delta}-1)}$$ Usage pipeline: $$\text{PAF}_{kd} =   \frac{R'_{K\delta}(OR_{K\delta} - 1)}        {1 + \sum_\delta R'_{K\delta}(OR_{K\delta} - 1)}$$ Pipeline:
code: # 1. Fit mortality OR per class   mort_or <- fit_mortality_rr_logistic(data, ...)    # 2. Assign OR to profiles via max rule (rr_col = "OR_death")   profiles_with_or <- assign_rr_to_profiles(       profiles_output,       rr_table = mort_or,       rr_col   = "OR_death"   )    # 3. Compute per-profile and overall mortality PAF   paf_mort <- compute_paf_rr_mortality(profiles_with_or) or_table          <- fit_mortality_rr_logistic(data, ...)   p0_res            <- compute_p0(data, ...)   rr_table          <- convert_or_to_rr(or_table, p0 = p0_res$p0)   profiles_with_or  <- assign_rr_to_profiles(profiles_output,                            rr_table = or_table, rr_col = "OR_death")   paf_mort          <- compute_paf_rr_mortality(profiles_with_or)

text: Named list, one entry per pathogen, list : profiles Data frame: profile (character label,     e.g.\ "RSR"), probability (\(\hat{p}_\delta\)),     one binary (0/1) integer column per antibiotic class indicating whether     class resistant (1) susceptible (0)     profile. classes Character vector antibiotic class names used     (alphabetical; bit 0 = classes[1]). n_classes Integer. Number classes used. constraint_residuals Named numeric vector     \(m_i^\top \hat{p} - v_i\) constraint. Small absolute     values indicate good constraint satisfaction.

title: Compute Resistance Profile Probabilities per Pathogen — compute_resistance_profiles
text: $$   \hat{p} = \arg\min_{p \\Delta_{2^n}}             \sum_{=1}^{m} \frac{(m_i^\top p - v_i)^2}{\sigma_i^2} $$ \(m = n(n+1)/2\) data-derived linear constraints encode n marginal resistance rates n(n-1)/2 pairwise co-resistance rates, \(\Delta\) standard probability simplex. QP solved via quadprog::solve.QP. small ridge term (ridge) added Hessian guarantee strict positive-definiteness. solver failure pathogen gets uniform distribution profiles.

title: Compute Resistance Profile Probabilities per Pathogen — compute_resistance_profiles
text: Marginal (rows 1 n) Row \(d\): \(M_{d,\delta} = 1\) iff \(\delta_d = 1\)       (.e., class \(d\) resistant profile \(\delta\)).       Constraint: \(\sum_\delta M_{d,\delta}\,p_\delta = \hat{r}_{kd}\). Pairwise (rows n+1 m) Row \((d_1,d_2)\): \(M_{d_1 d_2,\delta} = 1\) iff       \(\delta_{d_1} = 1 \land \delta_{d_2} = 1\).       Constraint: \(\sum_\delta M_{d_1 d_2,\delta}\,p_\delta =       \hat{r}_{k,d_1 d_2}\).       pairwise estimate unavailable (co-tested isolates),       product marginals (independence assumption) used fallback.

title: Compute YLL Associated with AMR (Patient-Level, Facility-Direct) — compute_yll_associated
text: every fatal patient pathogen k (optionally syndrome L) individual YLL contribution : $$\text{YLL}_{r,k} = \text{LE}(\text{age\_bin}_r, \text{sex}_r)   \times w_{r,k}$$ \(w_{r,k}\) polymicrobial death weight patient r pathogen k (= 1 monomicrobial, 0-1 polymicrobial episodes). Total YLL associated: $$\text{YLL}_{\text{associated}} = \sum_{r,k} \text{YLL}_{r,k}$$ Polymicrobial weights computed via flag_polymicrobial() + compute_polymicrobial_weight() weight.R.  facility_col provided weights derived per facility (reflecting local organism distributions), automatic fallback globally-pooled proportions facilities whose monomicrobial reference pool smaller min_mono_per_facility. date_culture_col NULL polymicrobial flagging skipped weights default 1. $$   YLL_K = \left[\sum_L \left(\sum_x D_L^x e_x^*\right) \times P_{KL}\right]           \times R_K^* $$ \(R_K^* = \sum_\delta R^*_{K\delta}\) (sum per-profile fatal resistance prevalences compute_fatal_resistance_prevalence). single syndrome (BSI): YLL_K = base_YLL x P_KL x R_K*. Also returns per-profile YLL_Kdelta = base_YLL x P_KL x R*_Kdelta.

text: list: total Scalar: total AMR-attributable YLL. per_pathogen One row per pathogen: pathogen_col,       n_patients, YLL_associated_k, PAF_k_mort,       YLL_attributable_k. by_age_sex YLL attributable stratified age_bin x sex. by_pathogen_age_sex YLL attributable pathogen x age_bin x sex. by_facility Per-facility breakdown (facility_col       supplied). by_syndrome Per-syndrome breakdown (syndrome_col       supplied). by_syndrome_pathogen Per-syndrome x pathogen (      syndrome_col supplied). stratified User-defined stratification (stratify_by       supplied). patient_data Patient-level data augmented PAF_kd       YLL_attributable_contribution. Named list: by_profile Data frame: one row per pathogen x resistant     profile R_prime, RR_dominant, excess_risk,     numerator_delta, denominator, MortPAF,     P_Lk, base_yll, YLL_Kdelta. by_pathogen Data frame: one row per pathogen     P_Lk, denominator, sum_MortPAF, YLL_K. total_yll Scalar: \(\sum_K YLL_K\). base_yll_used Scalar base YLL used.

title: Compute YLL Attributable to AMR — compute_yll_attributable
text: Two PAF modes: PAF_k scalar mode (default) resistance_profile_col     NULL, overall mortality PAF per pathogen k (PAF_k_mort)     paf_mort used scalar multiplier. Per-profile mode resistance_profile_col supplied,     patient row matched resistance profile delta     profile-specific PAF_mortality applied.  Patients whose     profile appear paf_mort receive NA     warning issued. $$\text{YLL}^{\text{attr}}_{,k} =   \text{yll\_contribution}_{,k} \times \text{PAF}_{k(,\delta)}$$ Eq. 15 – Mortality PAF per profile delta: $$   \text{MortPAF}_{K\delta} =     \frac{R'_{K\delta} \cdot (RR_{Kd^*}(\delta) - 1)}          {1 + \sum_{\delta'} R'_{K\delta'} \cdot (RR_{Kd^*}(\delta') - 1)} $$ Numerator per profile delta; denominator shared across resistant profiles pathogen K. sum \(\sum_{\delta'}\) runs resistant profiles (dominant_class != "all_susceptible"). Eq. 14 – Attributable YLL per profile: $$   YLL_{K\delta} = \text{BaseYLL} \times P_{KL} \times \text{MortPAF}_{K\delta} $$ Attributable YLL per pathogen: $$YLL_K = \sum_\delta YLL_{K\delta}$$

Data frame columns: pathogen, antibiotic_class,   OR_death, CI_lower, CI_upper, n_patients,   n_deaths, convergence_warning, syndrome_scope,   comorbidity_encoding.

title: Estimate Per-Class Mortality Odds Ratio via Mixed-Effects Logistic Regression — fit_mortality_rr_logistic
text: Resistance classified antibiotic class level (class = 1 drug class R), matching .build_class_resistance_wide(). HAI/CAI derived derive_infection_type_for_mortality(), prioritises explicit labels date-gap inference. Patients labelled "Known" excluded model (missing HAI covariate). ICU flag derived using "ever ICU" rule .derive_icu_binary(): patient flagged ICU = 1 row admission records ICU unit type. Comorbidity standardised .encode_comorbidity_mortality() (numeric Charlson, binary present/none, ordinal none/mild/moderate/severe). Pre-fitting checks per pathogen x class: min_n patients complete required covariates. min_deaths deaths (ensures outcome variation). Variation resistance (R S patients present). HAI/ICU collinearity (phi coefficient; phi_threshold).

what: Extract LOS Vectors by Resistance Status — get_los_by_resistance
title: Extract LOS Vectors by Resistance Status — get_los_by_resistance
text: given organism (optionally center), computes per-patient resistance status returns separate LOS vectors resistant susceptible exposures.

title: Extract LOS Vectors by Resistance Status — get_los_by_resistance
code: get_los_by_resistance(   abx_data,   los_data,   organism,   center = NULL,   patient_id_col = "PatientInformation_id",   organism_col = "organism_clean",   center_col = "center_name",   antibiotic_col = "antibiotic_name",   sus_col = "sus" )

title: Extract LOS Vectors by Resistance Status — get_los_by_resistance
text: abx_data Data frame antibiotic susceptibility results columns patient ID, organism, antibiotic name, susceptibility value (already cleaned "R"/"S"). los_data Data frame prepare_los_data(). organism Character. Organism name (lowercase, trimmed) filter . center Optional character. Center name filter . Default NULL (centers). patient_id_col Character. Patient ID column. Default "PatientInformation_id". organism_col Character. Organism column. Default "organism_clean". center_col Character. Center column. Default "center_name". antibiotic_col Character. Antibiotic name column. Default "antibiotic_name". sus_col Character. Susceptibility column (values "R"/"S"). Default "sus".

title: Extract LOS Vectors by Resistance Status — get_los_by_resistance
text: List elements R (LOS vector resistant exposures)   S (LOS vector susceptible exposures).

title: Get Magiorakos MDR/XDR Thresholds — get_magiorakos_thresholds
text: Returns pathogen-specific MDR/XDR classification criteria based Magiorakos et al. 2012 (Clin Microbiol Infect).

title: Get Magiorakos MDR/XDR Thresholds — get_magiorakos_thresholds
code: get_magiorakos_thresholds()

title: Get Magiorakos MDR/XDR Thresholds — get_magiorakos_thresholds
text: Data frame MDR/XDR thresholds per organism group

title: Get Magiorakos MDR/XDR Thresholds — get_magiorakos_thresholds
text: Magiorakos AP, Srinivasan , Carey RB, et al. Multidrug-resistant, extensively drug-resistant pandrug-resistant bacteria: international expert proposal interim standard definitions acquired resistance. Clin Microbiol Infect. 2012;18(3):268-281.

title: Get Organism Taxonomy Mapping — get_organism_taxonomy

code: get_organism_taxonomy()

title: Get Organism Taxonomy Mapping — get_organism_taxonomy
text: Data frame columns organism_name org_group

title: Get RR Pathogen Mapping — get_rr_pathogen_map

code: get_rr_pathogen_map()

title: Get RR Pathogen Mapping — get_rr_pathogen_map

what: Identify top N pathogens by occurrence — get_top_pathogens
title: Identify top N pathogens by occurrence — get_top_pathogens
text: Ranks pathogens number records (rows) dataset, optionally filtered specific syndrome, specimen, facility, outcome. Returns top N pathogens overall broken per facility.

title: Identify top N pathogens by occurrence — get_top_pathogens
code: get_top_pathogens(   data,   pathogen_col,   n = 5L,   syndrome_col = NULL,   syndrome_name = NULL,   specimen_col = NULL,   specimen_name = NULL,   outcome_col = NULL,   outcome_name = NULL,   facility_col = NULL,   facility_name = NULL )

title: Identify top N pathogens by occurrence — get_top_pathogens
text: data Data frame facility-level records. pathogen_col Character. Pathogen column. n Integer. Number top pathogens return. Default 5. syndrome_col Character NULL. Filter syndrome column. syndrome_name Character NULL. Syndrome value filter . specimen_col Character NULL. Filter specimen column. specimen_name Character NULL. Specimen value filter . outcome_col Character NULL. Filter outcome column. outcome_name Character NULL. Outcome value filter (e.g., "Died" "Discharged"). facility_col Character NULL. Facility identifier column. provided without facility_name, returns top N per facility. facility_name Character NULL. provided, restricts facility ranking.

title: Identify top N pathogens by occurrence — get_top_pathogens
text: Data frame columns: pathogen_col, n_records,   rank (1 = common), facility_col supplied.

title: Identify top N pathogens by occurrence — get_top_pathogens
text: Use decide pathogens focus calling calculate_P_Lk_prime_BSI(), calculate_cfr_lk(), calculate_YLD().

what: Check if Organism is a Contaminant — is_contaminant

code: is_contaminant(organism_name, syndrome = NULL, specimen_type = NULL)

title: Check if Organism is a Contaminant — is_contaminant
text: organism_name Character vector. Organism name(s) check. syndrome Character. Optional syndrome name filter contaminants. specimen_type Character. Optional specimen type filter contaminants.

title: Check if Organism is a Contaminant — is_contaminant
text: Logical vector indicating organism contaminant

title: Check if Organism is a Contaminant — is_contaminant

title: Select Resistance Class — select_resistance_class
text: Selects single resistance class per event using beta-lactam hierarchy relative risk (RR) values. Prevents double-counting burden estimation choosing clinically relevant resistant class.

code: select_resistance_class(   data,   event_col = "event_id",   class_col = "antibiotic_class",   susceptibility_col = "antibiotic_value",   rr_col = "rr_value",   hierarchy = NULL,   filter_resistant = TRUE )

title: Select Resistance Class — select_resistance_class
text: data Data frame resistance RR information event_col Character. Event ID column. Default "event_id". class_col Character. Antibiotic class column. Default "antibiotic_class". susceptibility_col Character. Susceptibility column. Default "antibiotic_value". rr_col Character. RR value column. Default "rr_value". missing, hierarchy used. hierarchy Named numeric vector. Custom hierarchy (class name -> rank). NULL, uses default get_beta_lactam_hierarchy(). filter_resistant Logical. TRUE, consider resistant (R) classes. Default TRUE.

title: Select Resistance Class — select_resistance_class
text: Data frame filtered one resistance class per event

title: Select Resistance Class — select_resistance_class
text: Selection logic: 1. Filter resistant classes (R) 2. Apply beta-lactam hierarchy (Carbapenems > 4GC > 3GC > ...) 3. Within hierarchy rank, prioritize RR value (higher RR first) 4. tied, select alphabetically reproducibility

title: Select Resistance Class — select_resistance_class
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Select single resistance class per event selected <- select_resistance_class(data)  # Include susceptible classes too selected <- select_resistance_class(data, filter_resistant = FALSE) } # }
